sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.2 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: For a subgroup$L$of a group$G$, let$L_G$denote the largest normal subgroup of$G$contained in$L$. If$M G$, then we say that$G$nearly splits over$M$if there is a subgroup$N G$such that$|G:N|=$,$|G:MN|<$, and$(M N)_G=1$. Let$G$be any group, and$H$a normal subgroup of prime order. Is it true that$(G) H=1$if and only if$G$nearly splits over$H$? % azarian@evansville.edu

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
